title: socks5_helper: ATYP=1 (IPv4) path never triggered by any test
description:
socks5_helper.rs handles ATYP=1 (IPv4 address) in its CONNECT parser (lines 54-58), consuming 6 bytes (4-byte address + 2-byte port). However, no test ever triggers this code path.

The only e2e test uses socks5h:// which causes tokio-socks to send a domain-name CONNECT (ATYP=3). There is no test that sends an IPv4 CONNECT (ATYP=1) through the helper.

This is a test coverage gap: if the IPv4 parsing branch had an off-by-one (e.g., reading 5 bytes instead of 6), the remaining unread byte would corrupt the subsequent bidirectional splice, but no test would catch it.
close_reason: ATYP=1 (IPv4) path works correctly per spec; the test helper uses whatever tokio-socks sends. P4 coverage gap accepted.
